security_gap.gap_description: Vote inputs may be spoofed without strong identity verification or signature checks.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Weak authentication allows impersonation for critical governance actions.

assumptions: The governance contract does not enforce strong cryptographic signature verification for vote messages.
Attacker can forge or replay vote messages.

mitigation.strategy: Implement cryptographic signature verification for all vote submissions.

description: An attacker may impersonate a legitimate user or BGT holder to cast unauthorized or fraudulent votes on governance proposals.

security_gap.gap_description: Vote messages lack sufficient integrity checks before processing.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Vote integrity relies solely on standard blockchain transaction integrity; application-level checks may be missing.

assumptions: Attacker can intercept and modify the vote transaction before it is included in a block.
The smart contract does not sufficiently validate the integrity of the submitted vote data.

mitigation.strategy: Ensure the entire vote payload is cryptographically signed and verified by the smart contract.

description: An attacker may tamper with the contents of a vote message (e.g., changing the vote selection or intended weight) before it is processed by the governance contract.

attack_surface.data_description: High volume of vote submissions or malformed vote messages

security_gap.gap_description: Governance contract or underlying blockchain is vulnerable to transaction flooding or resource exhaustion attacks.

actor_risk.description: User capable of submitting many transactions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Publicly accessible voting mechanism can be targeted by DoS attacks, potentially preventing legitimate participation.

assumptions: The blockchain network can be congested by a high volume of transactions.
The governance contract's gas limits or processing logic can be exploited to cause it to fail or reject valid votes under stress.

mitigation.strategy: Implement transaction prioritization based on fees and ensure robust smart contract gas handling; potentially require small staking for voting.

description: An attacker may flood the network or target the governance contract with a large volume of transactions to prevent legitimate users from casting votes or disrupt the voting process.

attack_surface.data_description: vote message including claimed voting power

security_gap.gap_description: Governance contract fails to correctly validate voter's BGT holdings/delegation or vote weight.

actor_risk.description: User attempting to gain unauthorized voting power.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Exploiting flaws in BGT balance checks or vote counting logic could grant unauthorized influence over governance decisions.

assumptions: The governance contract contains a bug in how it reads or validates the voter's BGT balance or delegated voting power.
The calculation or summation of vote weights is exploitable.

mitigation.strategy: Rigorous smart contract auditing and formal verification for BGT balance checks and vote counting logic.

description: An attacker may exploit vulnerabilities in the governance contract to cast votes with more weight than their BGT holdings/delegation allows, or influence the vote count unfairly.

attack_surface.data_description: Voter identity and vote selection made public on-chain.

security_gap.gap_description: Vote details (voter, choice) are immediately publicly visible on the blockchain upon submission.

actor_risk.description: Source of the public vote data.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Transparency of public blockchains makes vote details immediately visible, potentially impacting the voting process.

assumptions: The governance system does not implement a private voting mechanism or a commit-reveal scheme.
Real-time visibility of individual votes is considered a privacy or manipulation risk.

mitigation.strategy: Implement a commit-reveal voting scheme or explore zero-knowledge proof based private voting if vote privacy is a requirement.

description: Due to the transparent nature of the blockchain, a user's vote and identity (associated with their BGT address) are publicly visible immediately after the vote is cast, which could potentially influence other voters or expose voter preferences.
